rationale: Both candidates independently surface the same tangential match to the ground-truth concern (PR description claims trust_remote_code=True was added but it's absent from the diff/file), so recall is equal and only partial since neither identifies the actual kernels<0.15.0 version-compatibility mechanism the ground truth flags. Y's remaining findings (failure-not-cached correctness, cache-key correctness, and especially the lock-serializes-FA2/FA3-loads observation) are more technically substantive and accurately grounded than X's weaker docstring nitpick, though X's missing-cache-test suggestion is a reasonable, valid addition. Both are well-anchored with file/line references and concrete suggested actions, keeping actionability close, but Y edges out on precision/insight.
